heading: Government contributions

children.label: a
children.text: For each period in which an employee is insured under a policy of insurance purchased by the Office of Personnel Management under , a sum equal to one-half the amount which is withheld from the pay of the employee under shall be contributed from the appropriation or fund which is used to pay him.section 8709 of this titlesection 8707 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: When an employee is paid by the Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives, the Chief Administrative Officer may contribute the sum required by subsection (a) of this section from the applicable accounts of the House of Representatives.

children.label: c
children.text: When the employee is an elected official, the sum required by subsection (a) of this section is contributed from an appropriation or fund available for payment of other salaries of the same office or establishment.

Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, for each period in which an employee continues life insurance after retirement or while in receipt of compensation under subchapter I of chapter 81 of this title because of disease or injury to the employee, as provided under , a sum equal to one-half of the amount which is withheld from the employee’s annuity or compensation under shall be contributed by the Office from annual appropriations which are authorized to be made for that purpose and which may be made available until expended.section 8706(b) of this titlesection 8707 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall not be made other than with respect to individuals who retire, or commence receiving compensation, after ;December 31, 1989

children.label: B
children.text: shall not be made with respect to any individual for months after the calendar month in which such individual becomes 65 years of age; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall, in the case of any individual who elects coverage under subparagraph (B) of , be equal to the amount which would apply under this subsection if such individual had instead elected coverage under subparagraph (A) of such section.section 8706(b)(3) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: first becomes an annuitant by reason of retirement from employment with the United States Postal Service after ; orDecember 31, 1989

children.label: B
children.text: commences receiving compensation under subchapter I of chapter 81 of this title (because of disease or injury to the individual) after , if the position last held by the individual before commencing to receive such compensation was within the United States Postal Service.December 31, 1989
